region_of_origin: Malaysia (Malay Peninsula); variants in Indonesia and Singapore
food_subgroup: Traditional baked egg sponge cakes moulded in decorative brass acuan (fish, flower, star shapes); no added butter or oil — all fat from egg yolks

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI exists for kuih bahulu. Closest parent concept: sponge cake (FOODON:03301710).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code for kuih bahulu. Closest defensible parent: A039A (Cakes and pastries). Traditional preparation with brass moulds and egg-only fat profile is not captured in FoodEx2 as of 2026-04.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept for kuih bahulu or Malaysian egg sponge cake as of 2026-04.

preparation_method: batter poured into decorative fish/flower/star-shaped brass moulds

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of kuih bahulu is 8 small pieces (approximately 80g total, each piece ~10g), providing approximately 304 kcal, 48g carbohydrate, and 28g sugar per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Kuih Bahulu — IMR Malaysia MyFCD (kuih bahulu entry) cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy egg-and-flour sponge cake
nutrient_source.organization: Institute for Medical Research Malaysia (MyFCD) + USDA Agricultural Research Service SR Legacy
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Primary data from IMR Malaysia MyFCD kuih bahulu entry. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy used for cross-reference validation of egg-and-flour sponge cake macros and micronutrients. Both sources are on the approved list. Values are consistent between sources.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of egg sponge cake and refined-flour baked goods — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database sponge cake entries and peer-reviewed literature on refined-flour baked goods
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (analogous estimate)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for kuih bahulu in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI ~72 estimated from closest analogous food: plain sponge cake (GI 46–66 range in Sydney GI Database) adjusted upward to ~72 for kuih bahulu's airy crumb structure (expanded gas cells from whipped egg whites accelerate starch digestion), high sugar content (35g/100g), and refined white flour with no fat barrier from butter or oil. Glycemic load per 80g serving = (72 × 48g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 34.6 (HIGH). Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~120mg/100g; ~96mg per 8-piece serving)
hypertension.guidance: Kuih bahulu is one of the lowest-sodium traditional Malay kuih at ~96mg sodium per 8-piece serving, posing no direct sodium concern; however, patients should monitor total meal sodium from accompanying Raya dishes such as rendang and nasi kandar, which are typically high in sodium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~72) and high glycemic load (~34.6 per 8-piece serving) driven by refined white flour and 35g sugar per 100g
type2_diabetes.guidance: T2DM patients should limit kuih bahulu to a maximum of 4 pieces (~40g, GL ~17) per occasion, pair with unsweetened tea and a protein or fat source to moderate postprandial glucose absorption, and monitor blood glucose closely during Hari Raya festivities when cumulative consumption across multiple servings is common.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated cholesterol (220mg/100g from egg yolks) and moderate saturated fat (3g/100g); no trans fat; no added butter or oil
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: CVD patients should be aware of the high egg-yolk cholesterol content (220mg/100g) and moderate saturated fat; while kuih bahulu contains no added butter or oil and is lower in SFA than most baked kuih, portion control to 4 pieces per occasion is advisable, and total dietary cholesterol from all sources across the day should be monitored.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High glycemic load may contribute to postprandial inflammation; however, very low pro-inflammatory fat profile and egg-yolk Vitamin D supports immune regulation
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: RA patients may consume kuih bahulu in moderation (4 pieces per occasion); the high GL may contribute to postprandial inflammatory signalling, but the absence of added oils and the presence of Vitamin D (~1.2mcg/100g) from egg yolk partially offsets inflammatory risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kuih bahulu are traditional Malaysian small egg sponge cakes baked in decorative brass moulds (acuan bahulu) shaped like fish (ikan), flowers, and stars. The batter is made from eggs, sugar, all-purpose wheat flour, and baking powder — notably containing no added butter or oil, so all fat comes from egg yolks. The cakes are light, airy, and slightly crisp on the outside with a soft, spongy interior. They are a quintessential Hari Raya (Eid al-Fitr) kuih and are also enjoyed during Chinese New Year and as everyday snacks. Per 100g: 380 kcal, 8g protein, 11g fat (egg-yolk derived, 3g saturated), 60g carbohydrate (35g sugar), 0.5g dietary fibre, 120mg sodium, 220mg cholesterol. Per standard 8-piece serving (80g): ~304 kcal, 48g carbohydrate, 28g sugar, ~96mg sodium. GI ~72 (HIGH), GL ~34.6 per serving (HIGH) — the airy crumb structure from whipped eggs and refined white flour with no fat barrier causes rapid glucose absorption. Clinically: HIGH concern for Type 2 Diabetes due to high GL; LOW sodium concern for hypertension; MODERATE concern for CVD due to egg-yolk cholesterol. Source: IMR Malaysia MyFCD + USDA SR Legacy. Confidence: HIGH.
common_user_queries: how many calories in kuih bahulu

confidence_reason: Primary nutrient data sourced from IMR Malaysia MyFCD (exact kuih bahulu entry) cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for egg-and-flour sponge cake. Both are approved primary sources. Values are internally consistent. GI estimated at 0.9 confidence from University of Sydney GI Database analogous sponge cake entries with documented adjustment rationale.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for kuih bahulu not available in IMR Malaysia MyFCD or USDA SR Legacy sponge cake entries as of 2026-04. water_g set to -1: moisture content for kuih bahulu not reported in IMR Malaysia MyFCD as of 2026-04. trans_fat_g confirmed 0.0 — no hydrogenated fats used; no butter or margarine in traditional recipe. vitamin_c_mg confirmed 0.0 — no vitamin C sources in ingredients (eggs, flour, sugar, baking powder). GI ~72 is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database sponge cake entries adjusted for kuih bahulu's specific airy crumb structure and high sugar content; direct GI measurement for kuih bahulu not available in the Sydney GI Database as of 2026-04. Hindi local name 'कुइह बाहुलु' is a transliteration; no established Hindi culinary term exists for this Malaysian kuih. Kuih bahulu is a Malaysian dish consumed widely in Singapore; multilingual coverage follows Singaporean dish rules (English, Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) in addition to Indonesian (Kue Bahulu).
